transport.path: ~/.tmux-worktree/feishu-bridge-v1.sock; SHA-256 HOME fallback under tmp when the UTF-8 path exceeds 100 bytes

capabilityNegotiation.rollingUpgrade: a Dashboard may reuse a daemon only when the selected larkProfile and required capabilities match; an empty legacy daemon may be shut down and replaced; confirmed stale/pausing removal may migrate a legacy daemon only when it is the daemon's sole binding and no active turn would be disrupted
capabilityNegotiation.legacyCreate: fail with FEISHU_BRIDGE_UPGRADE_REQUIRED; never send sessionSummary to an old closed binding.create parser

eventSource.dashboardConversationPolicy: empty allowedSenderIds means any human user in the bound group may trigger only by mentioning the exact bot; bot/self, non-user, and unmentioned messages are ignored

reply.threading: reply in the source message topic; never fall back to a top-level chat reply

bindingLifecycleNotices.onCreate: sent after the exact binding and Feishu lease are durable
bindingLifecycleNotices.onManualRemove: sent after a live Feishu lease is released, or authority proves the target is FREE, TARGET_GONE, or held by a controlled local owner, and the binding is durably removed; missing lease plus Feishu/recovery ownership fails closed
bindingLifecycleNotices.legacyManualRemove: one-time removal of an active/paused binding through an already-running legacy daemon cannot emit this newer lifecycle card; Dashboard keeps that canonical legacy removal path to avoid interrupting its live authority, and later current-capability operations upgrade the empty daemon
bindingLifecycleNotices.onTargetGone: all persisted bindings, including paused and stale bindings, are reconciled by exact controlTargetId; only certain ended/replaced lifecycles are removed and announced
bindingLifecycleNotices.sameNameRecreation: never auto-retarget; announce replacement only after the old exact target is proven TARGET_GONE
bindingLifecycleNotices.uncertainTargetState: recovery, transport failure, or a new controlTargetId caused only by unknown controller continuity remains fail-closed and is not announced as deletion or replacement
bindingLifecycleNotices.delivery: best-effort ordered effect lane with deterministic idempotency; notification latency or failure never blocks lease renewal or rolls back terminal ownership or binding state

reactions.success: remove Typing; the reply card is the success signal
reactions.deleteFailure: do not add a contradictory CrossMark
reactions.authority: best-effort UX only; reaction failures never change terminal ownership or reply disposition
reactions.durability: reaction handles are bounded in-memory state; a hard daemon crash or uncertain create/delete acknowledgement can leave Typing until manually removed
